nocturnal-spirit-rx7.title: The Nocturnal Spirit
nocturnal-spirit-rx7.description: A hyperlime RX-7 FD3S, captured in full focus. Gloss carbon bonnet, half-raised pop-ups, vented hood and a race-ready stance. This isn’t just JDM royalty. It’s rotary defiance frozen in time. Powered by the iconic 13B twin-rotor, the RX-7 balances chaos and control beneath a body sculpted for speed and rebellion.
nocturnal-spirit-rx7.promo: Launch Special — 40% OFF The Nocturnal Spirit

images.alt: Framed Nocturnal Spirit RX-7 print on wall
Close-up of unframed RE:Volution RX-7 print slightly rolled
Nocutnal Spirit RX-7 print laid flat with kraft tube beside it

f488-unleashed.title: F488 Unleashed
f488-unleashed.description:
Back in 2018, Ferrari took the already outrageous 488 GTB and fine-tuned it into the 488 Pista. The name means ‘Track’ in Italian, and that’s exactly where this mid-engine monster belongs. More power. Less weight. Aero pulled straight from Formula 1. It wasn’t just some flashy, cash-grab, limited edition, it was Ferrari showing off what their mid-engined V8 could really do. That’s the Pista. 720 horses caged right behind your head, launching you to 100 km/h in under 3 seconds. 2.85, to be exact. The kind of acceleration that pins you to your seat and leaves you hanging on for dear life.

Seven years later, the Pista feels even more special. Today’s Ferrari’s are fast, no doubt, but with hybrid systems and sound filters, they don’t hit or feel the same. Back then there was flair, elegance, and a sense of real status – something you just don’t find anymore. Maybe some will call it nostalgia, but it's more than that. You can measure speed, but you can never measure feeling.

This particular Pista, finished in what I believe is Rosso Corsa, immediately caught my eye as I walked through the streets of Mont Kiara. Pista’s might not sit in that ultra-rare class, but when it looks this good, how can you not stop and appreciate it? What made the moment even more special was its backdrop of three to four story cement walls. The stark contrast between the Ferrari’s striking red and the muted monochrome structures only amplified its presence.

images.alt: Framed F488 Unleashed print on wall
Close-up of unframed F488 Unleashed print slightly rolled
F488 Unleashed print laid flat with kraft tube beside it

gt3-shadow.title: Escape the Shadows
gt3-shadow.description:
There’s nothing more haunting than a Porsche stripped of its luxury and filled with nothing but purpose.
But what happens when you put them wheel to wheel? That’s the Porsche Carrera Cup Asia. 26 top drivers from rookies to pros, fighting across six brutal circuits in identical GT3 Cups. Each one powered by a 4.0 litre flat-six pushing 510 horses and weighing just under 1300 kilograms. It’s loud, raw and built to dominate. And if you’re brave enough you could actually buy one straight from Porsche. One seat, no friends, no distractions, just you chasing 9000 RPMs.

Behind the wheel was Mathys Jaubert, a pro driver from France representing TORO Racing in Sepang where he secured his first ever win with TORO. Round 5. Heat shimmering off the tarmac, champagne in the air. His breakthrough weekend: two poles, three podiums and TORO back in the game.

images.alt: Framed Escape the Shadows print on wall
Close-up of unframed Escape the Shadows print slightly rolled
Escape the Shadows print laid flat with kraft tube beside it

yugata-rwb.title: RWB YUGATA
yugata-rwb.description:
You don’t need the golden hour when the car already looks like it. Named after dusk, the Yugata is the twelfth RWB built on Malaysian soil by the legend himself, Akira Nakai.
In June 2024, One Corsa came together with RWB Malaysia to reimagine the once-stock 993 Targa into what we see today. Finished in a deep Carmine Red, Yugata runs a set of OGW Fuchs-inspired rims, Moromito LED taillights and a fully custom interior that makes it truly bespoke, something synonymous with One Corsa’s attention to detail and craftsmanship.

images.alt: Framed YUGATA /// 012 print on wall
Close-up of unframed YUGATA /// 012 print slightly rolled
YUGATA /// 012 print laid flat with kraft tube beside it

hanabi-rwb.title: RWB HANABI
hanabi-rwb.description: Hanabi is the ninth RWB built in Malaysia. The name means “firework” in Japanese, a reference to its brief but striking presence when it first appeared. Built on a Porsche 993 Carrera 2, the car carries the defining traits of an RWB build. Wide arches shaped by hand form an aggressive stance that is unmistakably Akira Nakai’s work. This build was completed in late 2024 under RWB Kuala Lumpur in collaboration with Kuzig Glanz Detailing, alongside Malaysia’s tenth RWB, Kagayaki. Today, it rests at the owner’s car-inspired café, CARFE.

images.alt: Framed HANABI /// 009 print on wall
Close-up of unframed HANABI /// 009 print slightly rolled
HANABI /// 009 print laid flat with kraft tube beside it

miyabi-rwb.title: RWB Miyabi
miyabi-rwb.description:
Over nearly a decade, “Miyabi” has blossomed into a cornerstone of the Malaysian automotive scene. Built in August 2015, it marked the first ever RWB to be forged on the streets of Kuala Lumpur, and cemented itself as the fifth RWB in Malaysia. The choice of a cabriolet was quite unconventional as most owners gravitated toward coupes for rigidity. However, along with its illuminating Riviera Blue exterior and contrasting black interior, this 964-gen Carrera Cabriolet has aged like fine wine into one of Malaysia’s most recognisable wide-bodies. As of now, Miyabi rests in Carfé along with RWB Hanabi and Kagayaki, regularly receiving the admiration it deserves.

The name “Miyabi” signifies elegance and refinement, and unlike many of its RWB counterparts that scream aggression, this work of art wears its wide-body with finesse. From its understated aero ducktail to its literal ‘soft’ top, every detail feels intentional rather than excessive. Inside, it's all the driver with black Alcantara lined in red stitching, a Momo Propitopo steering wheel and Rennline pedals underfoot. For wheels, Miyabi rolls on a bespoke set of three-piece Work Meister M1s, featuring satin black centers and bare aluminium barrels. The owners who made this happen–Teoh, Kenneth and Zachary–were longtime RWB fans who even flew to Indonesia and Thailand to witness other builds before finally getting their turn. So more than a modified Porsche, Miyabi represents the moment Kuala Lumpur earned its place on the global RWB map and paved the way for nine more RWB’s to follow.

images.alt: Framed Miyabi /// 005 print on wall
Close-up of unframed Miyabi /// 005 print slightly rolled
Miyabi /// 005 print laid flat with kraft tube beside it

meisterwerk-m3.title: Wings of the 80s
meisterwerk-m3.description:
In the golden era of the 80s, the rules were simple: build 5,000 homologation road cars and the rest was up to you. That meant pure shithousery between the likes of Mercedes, BMW, Ford and whoever else had four doors with nothing to lose. Right in the middle of that beautiful chaos was the BMW E30 M3, a car that wasn’t the most powerful, the most expensive, or even the most extreme on the grid. So the engineers had to get a bit “creative”, like putting the fuel tank inches behind the driver’s head and calling it weight distribution, then deleting the ABS because who needs traction anyway. Then again, that’s easy to say when you have Roberto Ravaglia behind the wheel, the man who won it all in 1989 and brought Schnitzer their first ever DTM title.

Decades later, under all that legendary aura, the fuel tank is still in the back, the ABS is still missing, and nobody’s complaining about the “cheating” rear wing anymore. Which brings us to this replica displayed at BMW World 2025. Same red and blue stripes, same Warsteiner livery that won the 1989 championship. Calling it a “replica” doesn’t do it justice because this car wasn’t made just to be looked at. It has the same four-cylinder S14 engine – born from an M1 straight-six and a 14-day deadline, hence its name – and the desire to be driven on the track again. That’s exactly why it earned its place on our poster, “Wings of the 80s”.

images.alt: Framed Meisterwerk M3 print on wall
Close-up of unframed Meisterwerk M3 print slightly rolled
Meisterwerk M3 print laid flat with kraft tube beside it
